data.original: 你好 世界， 这 是 一个 测试 句子， 带有 多个 单词
data.tokenized_translation: Hello world , this is a test sentence , containing multiple words
data.grammar_explanation: This sentence is composed of two main clauses joined together. The first clause, "你好世界" (Hello world), is a greeting interjection directed at "世界" (world), functioning as a salutation. The second clause, "这是一个测试句子，带有多个单词" (This is a test sentence, containing multiple words), follows a standard Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) structure common in Mandarin Chinese. The subject is "这" (this), the verb is "是" (is/to be), and "一个测试句子" (a test sentence) serves as the predicate nominative. The classifier "个" (gè) is used after the numeral "一" (one/a), which is a grammatical requirement in Mandarin — nouns must be preceded by a measure word/classifier rather than an article as in English. The participial phrase "带有多个单词" (containing multiple words) acts as a modifier for "句子" (sentence), where "多个" uses the same classifier "个" to express plurality. Unlike English, Mandarin does not use articles (a, an, the) or inflect verbs for tense; instead, context and measure words convey quantity and specificity.
